links.targetTitle: Dispersed replicas are a one-field opt-in via bee-js RedundancyLevel

global.title: RedundancyLevel MEDIUM adds ~25% stamp cost via parity chunks
global.content:
## Stamp-cost accounting for `RedundancyLevel.MEDIUM`

The tradeoff is write-side, not read-side:

- **Stamp usage per write goes up ~25%**: MEDIUM adds ~1 parity chunk per 4 data chunks.
- **Op-batches (~1KB) still fit in one SOC**, so the overhead is negligible for `collab-ops-feed.ts:138`.
- **Larger chunks** (drive share bundle, manifest snapshots) see meaningful additional stamp consumption.
- **Reader side: zero runtime cost** if chunks arrive — redundancy is only consulted on miss/timeout.

**Required code change:** our existing stamp-utilization logic calls `calculateSize` / `calculateRemainingSize(encryption, ...)`. To report honest headroom, we must thread the `redundancyLevel` parameter through these calls: `calculateRemainingSize(true, MEDIUM)` rather than just `calculateRemainingSize(true)`.

**Rollback rule from the experiment plan:** if stamp-utilization chirps warn of exhaustion, back off to `OFF` for ops and keep `MEDIUM` only for long-lived bundles.
global.description: MEDIUM adds roughly 1 parity chunk per 4 data chunks. Must thread the redundancy arg into calculateRemainingSize/calculateSize so stamp-utilization logic sees the real cost.
